stories.headline: Supreme Court dismisses contempt petitions against critics of Duterte impeachment ruling
stories.summary: The Supreme Court en banc dismissed separate indirect contempt petitions filed against political analyst Richard Heydarian, Akbayan party-list Representative Percival Cendana and presidential adviser Lorenzo 'Larry' Gadon over their public criticism of the Court's ruling that struck down the impeachment articles against Vice President Sara Duterte. The Court said the petitioning lawyers failed to show a direct link between the statements and any real threat to the administration of justice.
stories.context: The Court's July 2025 ruling voided the original Articles of Impeachment against Duterte on procedural and due-process grounds, drawing public criticism from the three respondents that prompted Duterte-allied lawyers to seek contempt charges against them.

stories.headline: House says it is aligned with Marcos on keeping 2026 SONA simple
stories.summary: The House of Representatives said it is following President Marcos's directive to keep his fifth State of the Nation Address, set for July 27 at the House in Quezon City, austere but dignified, House Secretary General Cheloy Garafil said. About 1,000 guests are expected in the plenary and a similar number outside, with no red carpet or formal gowns permitted and attendees encouraged to wear simple Filipiniana attire.

stories.headline: Marcos expands cash aid to 7.5 million households amid Middle East-driven oil price hikes
stories.summary: President Marcos expanded the Uplift Assistance cash aid program to cover 7.5 million low-income households, or about 37.5 million Filipinos, through the Department of Social Welfare and Development, citing rising oil prices linked to the Middle East conflict. DSWD said on July 17 that beneficiaries include tax-paying 'near-poor' earners just above the poverty line, not only the poorest. The package gives a one-time P2,000 to 3.5 million 4Ps and Walang Gutom members, and monthly P2,000 from July to December to 2.5 million poor and near-poor households and 1.5 million low-income workers.
stories.context: The aid is a second wave under Executive Order No. 110's one-year national energy emergency declaration. The war between the US and Israel against Iran has driven up global oil prices and disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. DSWD Secretary Rex Gatchalian said the third beneficiary group covers Social Security System members earning up to P20,000 a month, and that selection used the Community-Based Monitoring System without political interference.

stories.headline: DepEd holds first active shooter drill at Manila Science High School
stories.summary: The Department of Education held its first active shooter drill at Manila Science High School, with about 1,770 students from grades 7 to 12 practicing lockdown, barricading and evacuation procedures under a 'run, hide, fight' protocol, the agency said. Education Secretary Sonny Angara said the drill is meant to be rolled out nationwide, with individual schools developing their own customized safety plans.
stories.context: The drill follows a June 22 shooting at a Tacloban City high school that killed three students and wounded 20 others, prompting DepEd to intensify school security measures nationwide.

stories.headline: Baguio marks 36th anniversary of deadly 1990 earthquake
stories.summary: Baguio City commemorated the 36th anniversary of the magnitude-7.7 earthquake that struck on July 16, 1990, killing about 1,600 people in the city, local officials said. Mayor Benjamin Magalong said the disaster prompted the city to build disaster-resilience systems and hold regular earthquake drills that did not exist before, while Representative Mauricio Domogan said unauthorized building modifications had contributed to structural collapses at the time.

stories.headline: P3.74-million worth of shabu seized in Camarines Sur buy-bust
stories.summary: The Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency in Bicol arrested three men in a buy-bust operation in Pili, Camarines Sur, seizing about 550 grams of suspected shabu worth P3.74 million, police said. The suspects, identified only by aliases, face charges under the Comprehensive Dangerous Drugs Act after allegedly being found with additional bags of the drug beyond what was sold to an undercover agent.

stories.headline: Southwest monsoon brings isolated rain to northern Luzon; no active cyclone in PAR
stories.summary: PAGASA said the southwest monsoon was affecting extreme northern Luzon as of its 4 a.m. bulletin, bringing isolated rain showers and thunderstorms to Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Abra, Batanes and the Babuyan Islands, with a risk of flash floods or landslides during heavier downpours. No tropical cyclone was active inside the Philippine Area of Responsibility, though a weak tropical depression was being tracked far east of Mindanao, outside the area.

stories.headline: Alas Pilipinas men stunned by Cambodia in SEA V Cup opener
stories.summary: Cambodia upset the Alas Pilipinas men's volleyball team in a five-set thriller, 23-25, 30-28, 20-25, 25-22, 15-8, in the opening leg of the 2026 SEA V Cup in Candon City, Ilocos Sur, dropping the Philippines to 0-1 in Pool A. Joshua Magalaman led the Philippines with 21 points, but Cambodia pulled away in the decisive fifth set after an early 4-0 run.
stories.watch_for: Alas Pilipinas is scheduled to face Indonesia next in Pool A action on July 17.

stories.headline: US and Iran press strikes into a seventh day as Gulf states come under fire
stories.summary: The United States completed a sixth consecutive night of strikes on southern Iran early on July 17, with Iranian media reporting hits on bridges, a railway station and other infrastructure in Hormozgan province, killing at least eight people and cutting power near Bandar Abbas, Al Jazeera reported. Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it launched a 13th wave of retaliatory strikes on US bases across the Gulf; Qatar reported a child hurt by falling shrapnel in Doha and sirens sounded in Bahrain, while Jordan said it intercepted three missiles.
stories.context: The conflict, which began in late February when the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, has repeatedly disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a waterway that normally carries about a fifth of the world's oil and gas supply. A June 17 US-Iran memorandum of understanding briefly paused the fighting before collapsing over disputes on how traffic through the strait should be managed.

stories.headline: 44 Japanese firms launch sovereign AI project with Nvidia chip order
stories.summary: Some 44 Japanese companies, led by SoftBank, Sony and Honda, launched a joint project to build a sovereign artificial intelligence foundation model, and Japan plans to purchase 27,500 Nvidia Rubin chips to support 'physical AI' development for robotics and manufacturing, Nikkei Asia reported. Japanese Economy, Trade and Industry Minister Ryosei Akazawa and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang were both involved in unveiling the initiative.
stories.context: The project is described as part of Japan's strategic push to protect its manufacturing competitiveness as the United States and China advance their own artificial intelligence capabilities.

stories.headline: Marcos promotes Philippines as digital investment hub after Singapore visit
stories.summary: President Marcos returned from a working visit to Singapore, where he pitched the Philippines as a destination for technology-driven investment at a Milken Institute roundtable and a meeting with Prime Minister Lawrence Wong, Manila Bulletin reported. Malacanang said firms including Singtel and ABC Impact reaffirmed plans to expand in telecommunications, data centers, digital infrastructure, AI and healthcare, and that Marcos cited the Luzon Economic Corridor, the PPP Code and Green Lanes for Strategic Investments as reforms to attract investors.

stories.headline: Philippines files diplomatic protest over China Daily depictions of Filipinos
stories.summary: The Department of Foreign Affairs said it lodged a formal diplomatic protest against Chinese state outlet China Daily over content it called demeaning and racist toward Filipinos, tied to the 10th anniversary of the 2016 South China Sea arbitral ruling. DFA Undersecretary Leo Herrera-Lim raised the issue with Chinese Ambassador Jing Quan on July 16, and the Philippine Embassy in Beijing wrote to the outlet's editor over an AI-generated video. National Security Adviser Eduardo Ano's office called the material a bid to discredit the ruling.
stories.context: The 2016 ruling by an arbitral tribunal in The Hague invalidated most of China's expansive maritime claims in the South China Sea; Beijing has rejected the decision. The protest comes ahead of Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi's expected visit to Manila for ASEAN meetings.

stories.headline: Ombudsman reopens 2019 SEA Games sports facilities case
stories.summary: Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla said he ordered a review of a dismissed case over alleged irregularities in the construction of sports facilities for the 2019 Southeast Asian Games, telling reporters his office is retrieving files to examine possible legal violations. The original complaint was dismissed in 2021 for insufficient evidence; Remulla said reopening it poses no double jeopardy issue because no charges were filed. The National Bureau of Investigation has separately said it will look into alleged anomalies in the projects.
stories.context: Public Works Secretary Vince Dizon was a subject of the original case in his former role heading the Bases Conversion and Development Authority, which oversaw New Clark City construction. Senator Alan Peter Cayetano, who chaired the 2019 SEA Games organizing committee, has said the NBI director tried to intimidate him ahead of a Senate appearance.

stories.headline: Oil firms expected to impose large pump price increases on July 21
stories.summary: Local oil companies are expected to raise pump prices sharply next week, with an industry source cited by GMA News estimating diesel could rise by P9.50 to P10.50 per liter and gasoline by P3.50 to P4.50 per liter effective July 21. The source attributed the increases to a higher geopolitical risk premium after Iranian attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz and US retaliatory strikes, reduced vessel traffic through the waterway, and lower Russian diesel exports. Final adjustments depend on end-of-week market averages.
stories.watch_for: Oil companies typically announce final price adjustments on Monday for implementation on Tuesday, July 21.

stories.headline: Metro Manila police monitoring four groups seeking protest permits for Marcos SONA
stories.summary: The National Capital Region Police Office said four groups have sought permits from Quezon City to hold protests during President Marcos's fifth State of the Nation Address on July 27, spokesperson Maj. Hazel Asilo said, adding that rally locations and expected turnout are still being finalized. The Philippine National Police plans to deploy more than 20,000 personnel and is still determining road closures around the Batasang Pambansa complex and along major Quezon City thoroughfares.
stories.watch_for: Marcos delivers his State of the Nation Address at the House of Representatives on July 27, with rally permits and road closures to be finalized before then.
